behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Validation/listing logic is a single local rule set. Failure boundary is inside one validator function (no module handoff required). Higher levels were rejected because behavior text does not require real I/O, model output, or cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the field-validation function with payloads missing 1..N required fields; assert it returns one error containing exactly all missing field names and a hard stop flag before downstream calls are invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure input-validation rules on one datum; real failure is local parsing/range logic. No required handoff, infra, or model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator with future date, >150-year-old date, malformed string, and valid boundary dates; assert correct reject/accept outcomes and error reasons.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets ...". Slot B: handoff between service -> API client -> response mapper. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a mapper-only test can miss wrong outbound payload field mapping or wrong wiring between request builder and interpreter. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior can be reproduced with in-process HTTP stub/contract test; no real network conditions required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake insurance service to real request builder and response interpreter against a local stub server; assert outbound payload fields (patient ID/plan code) and mapped internal states for each API response variant.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "API is unreachable ... marks eligibility as pending and continues the intake". Slot B: handoff/error propagation across insurance module into pipeline orchestrator. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, retry/fallback function might pass while orchestration still aborts intake on propagated exception. Rejection gate: System rejected because unreachable can be deterministically simulated via client timeout/connection-error stub without real network faults.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with insurance client configured to throw timeout/connection errors; assert eligibility becomes pending and downstream triage/scheduling steps still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload". Slot B: handoff from intake/context config -> LLM request composer. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, testing prompt-builder alone can miss dropped fields during orchestration/wiring. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because this behavior checks payload composition, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage call path with stubbed model endpoint; capture request payload and assert symptoms and rubric text are present and correctly sourced from config + patient report.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency ... emergency conditions". Slot B: model-output dependency (classification quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, stubbed responses always pass and cannot reveal misclassification by the real model. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior targets one model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative emergency symptom cases to the real triage model in repeated runs; assert urgency is high with acceptable consistency threshold and log misclassifications.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field". Slot B: handoff from parser output to persistence model/storage layer. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, parser may emit explanation correctly but repository mapping could drop the field. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior does not require judging explanation quality, only persistence of a field.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage-result ingestion through parser + repository against test DB; assert stored record contains explanation text and score, with explanation non-null/non-empty.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live AI ... explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale". Slot B: model-output dependency (generation quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, canned fixtures cannot detect generic/boilerplate explanations from the live model. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because only one agentic generation output is under evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Run real-model triage on varied symptom inputs; evaluate explanation content for symptom-specific references and rationale linkage, rejecting generic repeated templates.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "scheduled in the first available slot ... matches assigned urgency". Slot B: handoff between urgency output, scheduling policy, and calendar query/write modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, policy function can pass while repository ordering/query integration chooses wrong slot. Rejection gate: System rejected because local test DB/module wiring is sufficient; no OS/network primitive is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use seeded calendar data in test DB; invoke scheduler with high/medium urgency and assert chosen slot is earliest valid one under same-day/48h policy and persisted correctly.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "does not double-book ... when two patients are processed concurrently". Slot B: module+DB transaction/concurrency handoff boundary. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, locking/transaction race defects between scheduler and DB writes are missed. Rejection gate: System rejected because this can be caught with concurrent workers against the real local DB engine used in tests; no external network/OS-only primitive is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same slot against transactional test DB; assert exactly one booking succeeds and the other is re-routed/rejected per policy.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type". Slot B: handoff across appointment/patient data modules into message composer/template selector. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, template logic may pass while integration drops provider/time or selects wrong instructions for visit type. Rejection gate: System rejected because composing/validating message content does not require real transport infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Build confirmation via real composer pipeline using stored patient+appointment entities; assert rendered message contains all required fields and visit-type-specific preparation text.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Pipeline orchestration ... completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output". Slot B: cross-module orchestration/state handoff. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, individual step tests miss sequencing/state propagation bugs across stages. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected by explicit text "deterministic stubbed triage output" (disqualifies real model-output dependency). System rejected because deterministic local stubs are explicitly allowed.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end orchestration in-process with stubbed triage and payer adapters; assert stage order, state transitions, and final confirmation artifact are correct.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full live intake pipeline ... real patient submission" with live triage included. Slot B: model-output dependency in live triage within end-to-end flow. Slot C: if lowered to System, a deterministic/mock model could pass while real-model outputs break downstream assumptions or routing. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior does not require multiple distinct agentic decisions in sequence; only one agentic triage decision is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline against real payer API path and live triage model for a controlled real submission; assert autonomous completion and valid final appointment+confirmation artifacts without operator intervention.
